## [00:00 - 02:19]

Hey guys, just want to give a quick update. You just saw the previous video. So in this video, I'm going to show you the proof of concept. So the actual gig that you watched me apply to turned into a client. So since I recorded that video, it's been about two days. I sent that, it's Friday, I sent that Wednesday. She responded within a few hours. I put my link for her to schedule a call. We had a call the next day. I then followed up right here. And then today she actually sent an offer. So this offer is just sort of like a trial. We're just starting off with a couple things at first. But it basically comes out to be about 2000 a month. So nothing crazy, but it's a start. And it definitely can expand into other things. I got, essentially I got approval or confirmation of things go well. It could turn into anywhere between a 5 to 8k account. So obviously it's too much for a freelancer to handle, but I've got an agency. So yeah, it's great news. A lot of expansion potential here. What's wild is that I had preconceived notions on this account based on the budget, based on their past expenditure. And yeah, so just something to keep in mind that I want to express. It's so easy to jump to conclusions and to think, this is a bad sign, this person is, I don't know, because of this. And it's important to analyze that stuff. It's important to go, ooh, they're giving me a red flag. And if they give you so many red flags, cut it, cut it. But if they only give you one, don't shy away, right? The red flag that I had gotten was primarily that she wasn't paying much. She was paying other people, you know, 30, 40 bucks an hour. And here I was pitching 120, 100. And so I wasn't, you know, confident that she'd engage with that. And she even did talk about another offer she got with someone that was 30 an hour. But of course, if you prove expertise and you speak to the individual and build rapport, you can often close them at a much higher rate than other people. So yeah, just wanted to share that proof of concept that this is turned into now a client. So yeah, get on it.
